The '''Rneekii''' were a group of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] from the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Their [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] consisted of the [[MC40a light cruiser/Legends|MC40a light cruiser]] ''[[Sumpntodo]]'' and the [[Strike-class medium cruiser|''Strike''-class medium cruisers]] ''[[Ayenzii]]'' and ''[[Intombe]]''. Their forces were equipped with [[R-41 Starchaser]] [[Starfighter/Legends|fighters]] and [[Delta-class DX-9 stormtrooper transport|''Delta''-class DX-9 transports]].

==History==
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Rneekii attempted to capture the [[CR92a Assassin-class corvette|''Assassin''-class corvette]] ''[[Hashim group]]'' for its cargo of [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE Defender]] prototypes, using an [[Mine Type B|ion cannon minefield]]. Thanks to [[Tan (title)|Tan]] [[Maarek Stele/Legends|Maarek Stele]] and [[Alpha-class Xg-1 Star Wing/Legends|assault gunboat]] reinforcements, that attempt failed. During that same engagement, before the Imperials could launch Stele's craft from the hangar, the pirates seized an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] TIE Defender [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] from the [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class shuttle]] ''[[G'nabgib]]'' to hold him for ransom. Later, while the Imperials attempted to transfer the Defender prototypes from the disabled corvettes to [[Cargo ferry|cargo ferries]], ''Sumpntodo'' showed up to try to disrupt the party, but it was unsuccessful.<ref name="TIE Fighter">[[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']]</ref>

At [[Kiilimaar]], the Rneekii planned to sell the scientist to the highest bidder, which not surprisingly was the Empire. The exchange terms permitted the Imperials to send a unarmed shuttle and an assault gunboat escort, while the pirates had a half-dozen R-41 Starchasers accompanying the transport bringing the scientist. Stationed near the rendezvous point was ''Intombe'', whose hangar held more R-41s in reserve. However, it turned out to be a double-cross set by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], which was executed by Maarek Stele piloting the gunboat. The Empire tricked the Rneekii by handing over the ransom, retrieving the scientist, and then sending their code to attack the pirates, "full speed to Kiilimaar."<ref name="TIE Fighter" />

Alone for the opening part of the battle, Stele played an instrumental role in disabling the transport tasked with delivering the ransom to ''Intombe''. Taking full advantage of his Gunboat's advanced concussion missiles and the tractor beam weapon, the R-41s proved no match, each being quickly snared and blown apart by a single warhead. As the Strike cruiser launched R-41s to take out the Lambda shuttle, Stele fended off their numerous sorties to ensure the safe escape of the scientist.<ref name="TIE Fighter" />

Upon sending the code, the Imperials sprang the trap with the [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Interdictor Cruiser]] ''[[Red Claw]]'', accompanied by two [[Gamma-class ATR-6 assault transport|assault transports]]. The now-outnumbered Rneekii could not escape and were virtually wiped out, as their R-41s were shot down by Stele and the ''Red Claw''{{'s}} [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE interceptors]], while the ''Intombe'' was destroyed by [[Proton rocket/Legends|heavy rockets]] from the assault transports and [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bombers]]. Only a handful of Rneekii pirates—those with the Imperial ransom—survived, and they were captured by the Empire.<ref name="TIE Fighter" />

The fate of the Rneekii's other capital ship, ''Sumpntodo'', is unknown.
